data_quality_notes: Primary proxy (Statista WtE capacity) unavailable — paid subscription. Substituted with World Bank indicator 'World Bank SH.STA.HYGN.ZS: People using basic hygiene services (% of population)' as a proxy for environmental/waste infrastructure development. NOTE: This is NOT a direct measure of WtE capacity; it measures sanitation service access which correlates with waste management infrastructure broadly. Direct WtE capacity data was not available from any free/open API. EPA SMM energy recovery page accessible (HTTP 200). | EPA page references combustion facilities and energy recovery. | Statista URL (primary source) requires paid subscription; data not directly accessible.
summary: Hypothesis: Waste-to-Energy capacity positively correlates with WRR. Primary data (Statista WtE capacity) is behind a paywall. Used WB proxy 'World Bank SH.STA.HYGN.ZS: People using basic hygiene services (% of population)' as substitute. Pearson r=0.362 (p=0.0000), Spearman rho=0.294 (p=0.0003), n=145 observations. Verdict: partially_confirmed. Note: indirect proxy — sanitation infrastructure used as stand-in for WtE capacity.
proxy_substitution: Used 'World Bank SH.STA.HYGN.ZS: People using basic hygiene services (% of population)' instead of Statista WtE capacity data (paid/unavailable). Sanitation access is a correlated but indirect proxy.
